skill.name: reagent-expiry-alert
skill.description: Scan reagent barcodes or IDs, log expiration dates, and generate multi-level alerts before reagent expiry to support laboratory inventory management.
skill.summary: Track lab reagent expiration dates with barcode scanning and multi-level alert notifications.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: The static scanner flagged 32 potential issues across 4 files, but all 14 high-severity findings are false positives. The scanner confused JSON audit score data and markdown code fences with cryptographic content and shell execution. The only confirmed risks are standard local filesystem writes for inventory data storage (benign) and a GitHub source URL in documentation (legitimate attribution). No malicious intent, credential exfiltration, prompt injection, or code execution was detected.

low_findings.title: Local file storage in home directory
low_findings.description: The script stores reagent inventory data in ~/.openclaw/reagent_inventory.json, a hidden dotfile in the user home directory. This is a standard pattern used by CLI tools for local data persistence and is not malicious.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded GitHub URL in documentation
low_findings.description: SKILL.md contains a hardcoded URL to the GitHub source repository. This is standard attribution documentation, not a network exfiltration attempt.

low_findings.title: Markdown code fences misidentified as shell execution
low_findings.description: The static scanner flagged markdown code fences (triple backticks) as Ruby backtick shell execution. These are documentation code blocks showing CLI usage examples, not actual command execution.

dangerous_patterns.title: File write to home directory dotfile
dangerous_patterns.description: The script writes inventory data to ~/.openclaw/reagent_inventory.json using Python file I/O. This is a standard data persistence pattern for CLI applications and does not pose a security risk. Data is stored locally and never transmitted externally.

content.user_title: Track reagent expiration dates and prevent lab waste
content.value_statement: Laboratory staff often lose track of reagent expiration dates, leading to wasted materials and failed experiments. This skill logs reagent barcodes with expiry dates and generates multi-level alerts for safer inventory management.
actual_capabilities: Log reagent barcodes with names, expiration dates, locations, and quantities
Check for upcoming expirations with configurable alert windows (default 30 days)
Generate multi-level alerts: Expired, Critical, Warning, and OK status
View complete inventory sorted by expiration date
Emit warnings when logging reagents with past expiration dates
Provide reorder recommendations for expired and critical items
limitations: Does not support bulk import of existing inventory from CSV or spreadsheet files
Does not provide chemical hazard assessment or waste disposal guidance
Does not integrate with Laboratory Information Management Systems (LIMS)
Does not support controlled substance chain-of-custody tracking or regulatory compliance

use_cases.title: Lab Technician Intake
use_cases.description: A lab technician logs incoming reagent shipments by scanning barcodes and recording expiration dates for real-time inventory tracking.

use_cases.title: Weekly Expiry Audit
use_cases.description: A lab manager runs the alert check each week to identify reagents approaching expiration and schedule reorders before critical shortages occur.

use_cases.title: Experiment Preparation
use_cases.description: A researcher checks reagent availability and expiration status before planning experiments to avoid using expired materials.

prompt_templates.title: Log a New Reagent
prompt_templates.prompt: Scan reagent REAGENT-001 with name Tris Buffer, expiry date 2025-12-31, location Shelf A.
prompt_templates.scenario: Adding a new reagent to the inventory

prompt_templates.title: Check Expiration Alerts
prompt_templates.prompt: Check for all reagents expiring within 30 days.

prompt_templates.title: Full Inventory Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me the complete inventory sorted by expiration date and highlight any items expiring within 60 days.

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Reagent Logging with Alert Check
prompt_templates.prompt: Log reagent Tris Buffer (REAGENT-001) expiring 2025-12-31 at Shelf A and Sodium Chloride (REAGENT-002) expiring 2026-02-15 at Shelf B. Then check for all reagents expiring within 90 days.

output_examples.input: Scan reagent REAGENT-001 with name Tris Buffer, expiry date 2025-12-31, location Shelf A.
output: Alert Level: OK - expires more than 60 days from now
Recommendation: No reorder needed at this time

output_examples.input: Check for reagents expiring within 30 days.
output: ⚠️ Reorder Recommendation: Consider ordering Sodium Chloride immediately.

output_examples.input: Show me all inventory items sorted by expiry date.
output: Sodium Chloride (REAGENT-002) - Expires: 2026-06-01 - Location: Shelf B - Qty: 3
Tris Buffer (REAGENT-001) - Expires: 2026-12-31 - Location: Shelf A - Qty: 1
EDTA Solution (REAGENT-003) - Expires: 2027-03-15 - Location: Fridge C - Qty: 2

best_practices: Verify each expiry date against the reagent label before logging to prevent data entry errors
Run the alert check weekly to catch approaching expirations with enough time to reorder
Use consistent location names across all entries to make inventory navigation predictable
anti_patterns: Using this skill for chemical hazard assessment or safety data sheet lookup instead of its documented scope
Fabricating expiry dates or inventory counts instead of recording observed values from reagent labels
Using this skill to manage controlled substances that require chain-of-custody documentation

faq.question: What types of reagents can I track with this skill?
faq.answer: Any laboratory reagent with a known expiration date. The skill stores barcode, name, expiry date, location, and quantity for each item.

faq.question: Can I import my existing inventory in bulk?
faq.answer: No, the current version supports logging one reagent at a time. Bulk import from CSV or other formats is not yet available.

faq.question: What alert levels are available?
faq.answer: Four levels: Expired (past expiry), Critical (within 30 days), Warning (within 60 days), and OK (beyond 60 days).

faq.question: Can I customize the alert window from the default 30 days?
faq.answer: Yes, use the --alert-days parameter to set any number of days as your alert threshold for early warnings.

faq.question: Where is the inventory data stored?
faq.answer: Data is stored locally in a JSON file at ~/.openclaw/reagent_inventory.json on the machine where the script runs.

faq.question: Does this skill integrate with laboratory management systems?
faq.answer: No, it does not integrate with LIMS or other lab systems. All output is human-readable text without a structured data format.
